title: Tofu Tikka Masala
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://www.noracooks.com/vegan-tikka-masala/ ✓ VERIFIED
- **Description:** Tomato-cream curry with marinated, charred tofu cubes. Sauce is the star and bulk-preps; tofu finishes per ticket.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $2.40 / serving · **Menu price:** $14–17
- **Weight:** 360 g (sauce + tofu, no rice) · **Calories:** 480 · **Protein:** 22 g · **Time:** 45 min · **Servings:** 4
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — sauce holds beautifully; tofu chars in 90 seconds on a tandoor or salamander.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Soy curls / seitan | Chewier "chicken-style" | −$0.20 |
| **Premium faux-meat:** Beyond Chicken pieces / Chunk Pulled | Branded, premium menu listing | +$2.20 |
description: Tomato-cream curry with marinated, charred tofu cubes. Sauce is the star and bulk-preps; tofu finishes per ticket.
difficultyNote: sauce holds beautifully; tofu chars in 90 seconds on a tandoor or salamander.

subs.from: **Premium faux-meat:** Beyond Chicken pieces / Chunk Pulled

title: Rogan Josh (बिना हिंसा · Bina hinsa)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://holycowvegan.net/kashmiri-rogan-josh-vegan-version/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Holy Cow Vegan's recipe uses **chunky seitan and dried wild mushrooms** in cashew-yogurt sauce. Recommended: swap in Chunk Steakhouse Cut for the seitan to elevate to showstopper-tier presentation.)*
- **Description:** **Lead protein:** Chunk Foods Steakhouse Cut 6 oz, cubed and slow-braised Kashmiri-style with browned onions, dried Kashmiri red chili (for the famous deep red — no food coloring), cardamom, mace, fennel, ginger, and cashew-yogurt finish. The plant-based answer to the white-tablecloth Indian restaurant's signature dish. Plates with saffron pulao or naan.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $6.20 / serving · **Menu price:** **$22–26** (upscale Indian ÷ 0.27)
- **Weight:** 380 g · **Calories:** 540 · **Protein:** 32 g · **Time:** 60 min braise (sauce holds 3 days) · **Servings:** 4
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Medium** — sauce bulk-preps; Chunk pieces braise in 12 min from prep to plate.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Lead protein:** Chunk Foods Steakhouse Cut (default) | Best whole-muscle braise integrity | baseline |
| Beyond Steak Tips | Pre-seasoned shortcut, slightly less premium read | −$1.40 |
| Soy curls (rehydrated) | Budget tier | −$3.20 → menu $14–17 |
| Cubed paneer-style tofu | Vegetarian-Indian classic feel | −$3.40 → menu $13–16 |
urlNote: Holy Cow Vegan's recipe uses **chunky seitan and dried wild mushrooms** in cashew-yogurt sauce. Recommended: swap in Chunk Steakhouse Cut for the seitan to elevate to showstopper-tier presentation.
description: **Lead protein:** Chunk Foods Steakhouse Cut 6 oz, cubed and slow-braised Kashmiri-style with browned onions, dried Kashmiri red chili (for the famous deep red — no food coloring), cardamom, mace, fennel, ginger, and cashew-yogurt finish. The plant-based answer to the white-tablecloth Indian restaurant's signature dish. Plates with saffron pulao or naan.
difficultyNote: sauce bulk-preps; Chunk pieces braise in 12 min from prep to plate.

subs.effect: Pre-seasoned shortcut, slightly less premium read

title: Gulab Jamun (बिना हिंसा · Bina hinsa)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://holycowvegan.net/vegan-gulab-jamun/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Holy Cow Vegan — uses bread + cashew cream as the vegan khoya substitute)*
- **Description:** Fried milk-solid dough balls — built here with non-dairy milk powder + chickpea flour + semolina — soaked in cardamom-rose-saffron syrup. The most-requested Indian sweet. Holds beautifully in syrup for 3+ days; only improves.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $1.05 / 2-piece portion (~$0.55/piece) · **Menu price:** $7–10 (3-piece) / $5–7 (2-piece)
- **Weight:** 90 g / 2-piece portion · **Calories:** 320 · **Protein:** 5 g · **Time:** 60 min · **Servings:** ~16 pieces / batch
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Medium** — fry dough balls (timing matters — they crack if oil is wrong), then steep in warm syrup. Once made, holds in syrup at room temp 1 day or refrigerated 5 days.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service (warm to order)

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Non-dairy milk powder + chickpea flour (default) | Authentic mouthfeel | baseline |
| Add saffron-pistachio kulfi alongside | Premium duo plate | +$1.20 → menu $11–14 |
urlNote: Holy Cow Vegan — uses bread + cashew cream as the vegan khoya substitute
description: Fried milk-solid dough balls — built here with non-dairy milk powder + chickpea flour + semolina — soaked in cardamom-rose-saffron syrup. The most-requested Indian sweet. Holds beautifully in syrup for 3+ days; only improves.
difficultyNote: fry dough balls (timing matters — they crack if oil is wrong), then steep in warm syrup. Once made, holds in syrup at room temp 1 day or refrigerated 5 days.
